message:
fix(cancel): use u64::MAX sentinel for unset cancelled_at timestamps

Change the sentinel value for an uncancelled token's cancelled_at field from 0
to u64::MAX. Time(0) is a valid timestamp (epoch start), so using 0 as the
"not yet cancelled" marker meant that a token cancelled at exactly Time(0)
was indistinguishable from an uncancelled token. u64::MAX is not a realistic
nanosecond timestamp and eliminates this ambiguity.

Affected locations:
- SymbolCancelToken::new() and ::with_budget() initializers
- SymbolCancelToken::from_raw() deserialization path
- SymbolCancelToken::new_detached() factory
- cancelled_at() reader: now checks for u64::MAX instead of 0, with a
  defensive fallback (returns None if synced value is still u64::MAX after
  taking the reason lock, covering the from-bytes case where reason is
  never set)

Also fixes a potential overflow in CleanupCoordinator: replace plain addition
with saturating_add for total_bytes accumulation to prevent wrapping under
extreme cleanup load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
